mnemonics.title: Fogg's B=MAT
mnemonics.content: Remember: B = MAT. Just think of a math formula! Behavior happens when Motivation and Ability multiply with Trigger. All three must be present.

mnemonics.title: Hook Model Order
mnemonics.content: TAVI - Trigger Action Variable Investment. Think of a piano keyboard! T-A-V-I in sequence.

mnemonics.title: Dark Patterns Types
mnemonics.content: STAFF: Sneak into basket, Forced action, Asymmetric choice, Moving goalposts, Fine print. Like sneaky STAFF members!

mnemonics.title: Cognitive Biases Memory
mnemonics.content: SCAN: Scarcity, Cognitive load, Anchoring, Nudge. Like scanning your memory!

mnemonics.title: Visual Hierarchy Order
mnemonics.content: SIZE + POSITION + COLOR + CONTRAST = Focus. Big top-left bold items grab attention first.

acronyms.usage: Fogg's model for predicting user behavior. All three must be present for action.
The four phases of the Hook Model for creating habit-forming products.

acronyms.expansion: Sneak into basket, Trick questions, Asymmetric, Forced continuity, Fine print
acronyms.usage: Types of Dark Patterns that trick users into unwanted actions.
The overall experience of a user when interacting with a product or service.
The visual elements through which users interact with a product.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a newspaper headline. Biggest, boldest, highest contrast = most important. Users scan in F-pattern: top-left to right, then down left side.
Think of a triangle with three sides: Motivation, Ability, Trigger. All three must meet at a point for behavior to occur. Like a three-legged stool!
A circular cycle: Trigger (bell) → Action (hammer) → Variable Reward (treat) → Investment (add more bells). Like training a pet with rewards!
Think of a computer's RAM. Full screen = slow performance. Clean interface = fast. Don't overload the 'mental RAM'!
Weighing scale: Losses feel like 2.5kg, gains feel like 1kg. Users avoid moving the loss side down!

analogies.analogy: Like an alarm clock - it prompts you to wake up. External triggers (notifications) and internal triggers (emotions) prompt action.
Like a slot machine - unpredictable rewards are more addictive than predictable ones. Social media likes work the same way!
Like a kitchen counter. Cluttered counter = hard to cook. Clear counter = easy to work. Keep interfaces 'clean'.
Like a sneaky car salesman who adds hidden fees. Not illegal but manipulative and damages trust.
Like超市 placing healthy food at eye level. You're not forced, but guided toward better choices.
